name: Leopold Stokowski
abstract: Leopold Anthony Stokowski (UK:  stə-KOF-skee, US:  stə-KAWF-skee, stə-KOW-skee; 18 April 1882 – 13 September 1977) was a British conductor. One of the leading conductors of the early and mid-20th century, he is best known for his long association with the Philadelphia Orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: John Beal
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Leopold Stokowski and that was a lot of fun. ... I was fortunate enough to get principal bass in his orchestra
about_quotes.doc_title: John Beal interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: invited to conduct the Philadelphia Orchestra by Leopold Stokowski, who was the conductor of that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Leopold Stokowski... took me aside... And he said he’d like to have me with his orchestra down in Houston
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 17, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: on my dossier I write charter member of the American Symphony Orchestra with Leopold Stokowski
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: We had to get Leopold Stokowski (PHONETIC) out of the line. He was standing around the corner in the snow. We found out he was in line. We went around and got him out of the line and gave him a box in the theater.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: Leopold Stokowski. Figure that one out. Yeah, and you know who used to come in and sit right... and sit right in front so they could watch Leo? Jack Benny and his wife, and Robert Taylor and Barbara Stanwyck.
